title: Describing People and Things Game Zone
source: Generated from approved unit runtime and linked master dictionary

games.title: Meaning Match
games.description: Match each unit word to its approved child-friendly meaning.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: Nice and helpful to other people.
rounds.answer: kind
rounds.explanation: kind: Nice and helpful to other people.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: Doing many things and having little free time.
rounds.answer: busy
rounds.explanation: busy: Doing many things and having little free time.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: Hard or bumpy; not smooth.
rounds.answer: rough
rounds.explanation: rough: Hard or bumpy; not smooth.

games.title: Spelling Builder
games.description: Choose letter tiles to build important unit words.

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: Happy to talk to people and play with them.
rounds.answer: friendly

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: Quiet and gentle, and not angry or excited.
rounds.answer: calm

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: Strong and hard to break, tear or hurt.
rounds.answer: tough

games.title: Sentence Puzzle
games.description: Put unit words into the correct sentence order.

rounds.prompt: Build the complete model sentence.
rounds.answer: The kind girl shared her lunch with a new pupil.

rounds.prompt: Build the complete model sentence.
rounds.answer: Amal is honest, so she told the teacher what really happened.

rounds.prompt: Build the complete model sentence.
rounds.answer: The teacher was too busy to talk right now.

games.title: Language Choice
games.description: Choose the form that makes the unit language accurate.

rounds.prompt: What is the correct comparative form of the adjective happy?
rounds.answer: happier
rounds.explanation: Words ending in -y change the y to i before -er, so happy becomes happier.

rounds.prompt: Which sentence uses could correctly?
choices: She could to play the drum.
rounds.answer: She could play the drum.
rounds.explanation: Could is always followed by the plain verb, so play takes no -s, no -ed and no to.

rounds.prompt: Teacher Yasmin wants to know the reason for something. Which question word should she use?
rounds.answer: Why
rounds.explanation: Why asks for a reason, where asks about a place, when asks about a time and who asks about a person.

games.title: Reading Detective
games.description: Use unit evidence to solve each reading clue.

rounds.prompt: Who is the writer's favourite person in ‘My Cousin Noah’?
choices: The writer's favourite person is her cousin Noah.
Any three of these words: kind, honest, busy, calm and friendly.
The speaker was sad, and Nora stayed calm and helped her.
rounds.answer: The writer's favourite person is her cousin Noah.
rounds.explanation: Review the unit text evidence: The writer's favourite person is her cousin Noah.

rounds.prompt: What is the first road like, and what is the second road like?
choices: Any three of these words: kind, honest, busy, calm and friendly.
He could carry ten bags of rice.
The first road is smooth and easy to walk on, and the second road is rough with bumps and stones.
rounds.answer: The first road is smooth and easy to walk on, and the second road is rough with bumps and stones.
rounds.explanation: Review the unit text evidence: The first road is smooth and easy to walk on, and the second road is rough with bumps and stones.

rounds.prompt: How did Nora help the speaker yesterday?
choices: She was new, because her family had moved to the town, so nobody knew her name yet.
The speaker was sad, and Nora stayed calm and helped her.
The first road is smooth and easy to walk on, and the second road is rough with bumps and stones.
rounds.answer: The speaker was sad, and Nora stayed calm and helped her.
rounds.explanation: Review the unit text evidence: The speaker was sad, and Nora stayed calm and helped her.

games.title: Speaking Quest
games.description: Listen, record, review and submit useful unit language.

rounds.prompt: Speaking 1 — Describe Your Favourite Person
Speaking 3 — Guess Who
rounds.target: This person is kind, busy and honest.

rounds.prompt: Speaking 5 — Explain and Respond
rounds.target: Explain why Nora is kinder than she believes she is.

games.title: Word Order Race
games.description: Arrange unit language in a clear, accurate order.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: A careless move knocked the cup off the table.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: The road was very rough after the heavy rain.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: The little fish is still alive in the bucket of water.

games.title: Definition Dash
games.description: Choose the approved meaning before moving to the next clue.

rounds.prompt: What does 'calm' mean in this unit?
choices: Quiet and gentle, and not angry or excited.
Nice and helpful to other people.
Happy to talk to people and play with them.
rounds.answer: Quiet and gentle, and not angry or excited.
rounds.explanation: calm: Quiet and gentle, and not angry or excited.

rounds.prompt: What does 'similar' mean in this unit?
choices: Nice and helpful to other people.
Happy to talk to people and play with them.
Almost the same as something else, but not exactly.
rounds.answer: Almost the same as something else, but not exactly.
rounds.explanation: similar: Almost the same as something else, but not exactly.

rounds.prompt: What does 'friendly' mean in this unit?
choices: Always telling the truth, even when it is hard.
Happy to talk to people and play with them.
Nice and helpful to other people.
rounds.answer: Happy to talk to people and play with them.
rounds.explanation: friendly: Happy to talk to people and play with them.

games.title: Word Type Power
games.description: Identify how each vocabulary word works in English.

rounds.prompt: What type of word is 'popular'?
rounds.answer: Adjective
rounds.explanation: popular is used as a adjective in this dictionary sense.

rounds.prompt: What type of word is 'favourite'?
rounds.answer: Adjective
rounds.explanation: favourite is used as a adjective in this dictionary sense.

rounds.prompt: What type of word is 'care'?
rounds.answer: Noun
rounds.explanation: care is used as a noun in this dictionary sense.

games.title: Memory Pairs
games.description: Reveal tiles and connect each word with its meaning.

rounds.prompt: Match vocabulary set 1.
pairs: Always telling the truth, even when it is hard.
The act of being kind and helpful to somebody.
Doing many things and having little free time.

rounds.prompt: Match vocabulary set 2.
pairs: Having the skill or the strength to do something.
Liked and chosen by many different people.

rounds.prompt: Match vocabulary set 3.
pairs: Almost the same as something else, but not exactly.
The one you like the most of all.
Nice and helpful to other people.

games.title: Question Quest
games.description: Read each unit question carefully and choose the best answer.

rounds.prompt: Choose the missing word from ‘Two Roads’: My father says his old boots are _____, so the rough road is not a problem.
rounds.answer: tough
rounds.explanation: Only tough describes an object, because honest, popular and calm all describe people.

rounds.prompt: In the story, which two kind acts did Amal write about in her notebook?
choices: Nora caught the papers and closed the gate.
Nora gave back a fallen bag and helped a boy who fell.
Nora carried ten heavy bags of rice and cleaned every window.
Nora shared her lunch and mended a broken chair.
rounds.answer: Nora gave back a fallen bag and helped a boy who fell.
rounds.explanation: Amal's notebook names the fallen bag and the boy who tripped in the garden, and nothing else.

rounds.prompt: Which sentence uses the past simple correctly?
choices: He goed to the shop yesterday.
He gone to the shop yesterday afternoon.
He went to the shop yesterday.
He go to the shop yesterday.
rounds.answer: He went to the shop yesterday.
rounds.explanation: Go is an irregular verb, so its past simple is went rather than goed or gone.

games.title: Unit Mission
games.description: Finish with a mixed challenge across the whole unit.

rounds.prompt: What does the word kindness mean?
choices: The act of being kind and helpful to somebody.
A rough road with many bumps, stones and puddles.
A person who is liked by many other people.
Something you are able to do without any help.
rounds.answer: The act of being kind and helpful to somebody.
rounds.explanation: Kindness names the action, while kind is the adjective that describes the person doing it.

rounds.prompt: Which meaning matches the adjective tough?
choices: Almost the same as something else, but not exactly.
Strong and hard to break, tear or hurt.
Living and growing, and not dead.
Nice and helpful to other people.
rounds.answer: Strong and hard to break, tear or hurt.
rounds.explanation: Tough describes strength, so boots and gloves are tough, while rough describes a bumpy surface.

rounds.prompt: Choose the missing word from ‘My Cousin Noah’: He is always _____, but he still has time to help me.
rounds.answer: busy
rounds.explanation: The text says Noah is always busy, and the word but shows the surprise that he still finds time.
